chapter_context.summary: Laws regarding unsolved murders, marriage to captives, and rebellious sons.

outline.explanation: This section focuses on Unsolved Murders (21:1-9), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Deuteronomy.
This section focuses on Marriage to Female Captives (21:10-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Deuteronomy.
This section focuses on Inheritance of the Firstborn (21:15-17), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Deuteronomy.
This section focuses on Rebellious Sons and Cursed Hanging (21:18-23), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Deuteronomy.

key_themes.title: Covenant Loyalty
key_themes.description: The requirement to love and obey God from the heart.

key_themes.title: Remembrance
key_themes.description: The importance of recalling God's acts to maintain faith.

devotional.question: How can we 'circumcise our hearts' today as Moses commanded the Israelites?
What blessings have you seen in your life as a result of choosing obedience?

cultural_historical.title: The Second Law
cultural_historical.text: Deuteronomy is not a new law but a 're-iteration' (deutero-nomos) for the generation about to enter the land.
